project_summary: Autonomous deep-loop research sprint for New Topic.
research_goal: Understand the New Topic sector well enough to pick a narrow product wedge, define the correct user, and generate a lowering pass that can drive a factory build.
core_question: What specific product should be built in New Topic, for which user, and what are the minimum primitives required for a credible first release?
atomic_win: A concrete product wedge with evidence-backed scope, constraints, and implementation sequencing.

output_target.title: Evidence-Backed Product Spec
output_target.description: Produce a research synthesis that is specific enough to drive a lowering pass and factory build without relying on guesswork.
points: who the target buyer or operator is
which workflow pain matters enough to solve now
which competitors define the market frame
which narrow MVP wedge is worth building first
which primitives and constraints must survive the lowering pass

directives.title: Market Structure
directives.overview: Understand the sector shape, spending logic, and where this market is already crowded or weak.
questions: Who are the main buyer segments in this market?
What problem categories are they already paying to solve?
Which market shifts or platform changes make this topic newly interesting?
Where is the market fragmented versus concentrated?
What adjacent categories compete for the same budget?

directives.title: User Workflow and Pain
directives.overview: Map the real workflow, the expensive bottlenecks, and the moments where a product could create leverage.
questions: What does the current workflow look like before software intervention?
Where do users lose time, money, or trust in that workflow?
Which steps require judgment versus rote coordination?
Which pain points are urgent enough to justify adoption?
Which parts of the workflow must remain human-reviewed?

directives.title: Competitive Landscape
directives.overview: Identify direct and adjacent competitors, their wedge, and where they leave obvious space open.
questions: Who are the direct competitors for this workflow?
How are they positioned and priced?
What capabilities are table stakes versus differentiators?
What do public docs, reviews, or demos suggest users still dislike?
Which adjacent tools solve part of the job but not the full workflow?

directives.title: Product Definition
directives.overview: Turn the research into a product wedge, a primitive model, and a buildable scope boundary.
questions: What is the best one-run MVP wedge for this market?
Which user and use case should be explicitly excluded from v1?
What canonical entities or primitives does the product need?
What technical or legal constraints materially affect scope?
What should go into the lowering pass so the factory compiler builds the right thing?

agent_groups.name: agent-1
agent_groups.start_question: Who are the main buyer segments in this market?

agent_groups.name: agent-2
agent_groups.start_question: What does the current workflow look like before software intervention?

agent_groups.name: agent-3
agent_groups.start_question: Who are the direct competitors for this workflow?

agent_groups.name: agent-4
agent_groups.start_question: What is the best one-run MVP wedge for this market?
